content:
# Deuteronomy Chapter 7 — The EMET Bible (Literal)
**Translator:** EMET | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), Folios 204–205 | **Source Images:** source-texts/hebrew-OT/DEU_007_page_001.jpg, DEU_007_page_002.jpg | **LXX:** Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 148–149

---

1 When the LORD your God brings you into the land where you are going in to possess it, and he drives out many nations from before you — the Hittite and the Girgashite and the Amorite and the Canaanite and the Perizzite and the Hivite and the Jebusite — seven nations greater and more numerous than you,

2 and the LORD your God gives them before you, and you strike them down — you shall utterly devote them to destruction [cherem]; you shall make no covenant with them and you shall show them no favor.

3 And you shall not intermarry with them; your daughter you shall not give to his son, and his daughter you shall not take for your son.

4 For he would turn your son away from following me, and they would serve other gods; then the anger of the LORD would burn against you and he would destroy you quickly.

5 But thus shall you deal with them: their altars you shall tear down, and their pillars [masseboth] you shall smash, and their Asherah-poles you shall hew down, and their carved images you shall burn with fire.

6 For you are a holy people [am qadosh] to the LORD your God; you the LORD your God has chosen to be for him a treasured people [am segullah] from all the peoples who are upon the face of the ground.

7 Not because you were more numerous than all the peoples did the LORD set his love upon you and choose you — for you were the fewest of all the peoples —

8 but because of the LORD's love for you and his keeping of the oath which he swore to your fathers, the LORD brought you out with a strong hand and redeemed you from the house of slavery, from the hand of Pharaoh king of Egypt.

9 And you shall know that the LORD your God, he is God, the faithful God, keeping the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness [hesed] to those who love him and keep his commandments, to a thousand generations,

10 and repaying those who hate him to their face, to destroy them; he will not be late with the one who hates him — to his face he will repay him.

11 And you shall keep the commandment and the statutes and the ordinances which I am commanding you today, to do them.

12 And it shall be, because you heed these ordinances and keep and do them, that the LORD your God will keep with you the covenant and the covenant-faithfulness [hesed] which he swore to your fathers.

13 And he will love you and bless you and multiply you, and he will bless the fruit of your womb and the fruit of your ground, your grain and your new wine and your fresh oil, the offspring of your cattle and the young of your flock, upon the ground which he swore to your fathers to give you.

14 You shall be blessed above all peoples; there shall not be in you a barren male or a barren female, nor among your livestock.

15 And the LORD will turn away from you every sickness; and all the evil diseases of Egypt, which you knew, he will not put upon you, but he will lay them upon all who hate you.

16 And you shall consume all the peoples which the LORD your God is giving over to you; your eye shall not look with pity upon them; and you shall not serve their gods, for that would be a snare to you.

17 If you say in your heart, "These nations are more numerous than I — how am I able to dispossess them?"

18 you shall not be afraid of them; you shall well remember what the LORD your God did to Pharaoh and to all Egypt:

19 the great trials which your eyes saw, and the signs and the wonders and the strong hand and the outstretched arm by which the LORD your God brought you out — so shall the LORD your God do to all the peoples of whom you are afraid.

20 And also the hornets [tsir'ah] the LORD your God will send among them, until those who are left and who hide themselves perish from before you.

21 You shall not tremble before them, for the LORD your God is in your midst, a great and awesome God.

22 And the LORD your God will clear away these nations before you little by little; you will not be able to finish them off quickly, lest the wild animals become too numerous against you.

23 And the LORD your God will give them before you and will throw them into great confusion until they are destroyed.

24 And he will give their kings into your hand, and you shall make their name perish from under the heavens; no man shall stand before you until you have destroyed them.

25 The carved images of their gods you shall burn with fire; you shall not covet the silver or the gold that is on them and take it for yourself, lest you be snared by it, for it is an abomination [to'evah] to the LORD your God.

26 And you shall not bring an abomination into your house and become devoted to destruction [cherem] like it; you shall utterly loathe it and utterly abhor it, for it is a devoted thing [cherem].

---

## EMET's Textual Notes

**V.1 — Seven nations:** The MT lists seven nations as is standard for this formula. The LXX (Vat.gr.1209 p.148, CAP. VII) lists the same seven nations in a slightly varied order, placing Γεργεσαῖον (Girgashite) in a position that differs from some MT manuscripts; the Leningrad Codex (LC, p.204) preserves the standard MT sequence. This chapter spans LC pages 204–205.

**V.2 — cherem:** Hebrew חֵרֶם (cherem) — "utter devotion to destruction," the ban of holy war. The LXX renders ἀναθεματιεῖτε (you shall anathematize/put under the ban). "Utterly devote to destruction" preserves the covenantal-legal force; simple "destroy" would lose the sacred dimension.

**V.6 — am segullah:** Hebrew עַם סְגֻלָּה — "a treasured people," lit. "a people of special possession/property." The LXX renders λαὸν περιούσιον (a people of special possession). Not translated as "special treasure" (too commercial); "treasured people" preserves the relational nuance.

**V.8 — hesed:** Hebrew חֶסֶד — rendered "covenant-faithfulness" per established EMET convention. The LXX renders ἐλέους (mercy/compassion). The Hebrew term encompasses steadfast covenant loyalty; "mercy" alone is insufficient.

**V.9 — "to a thousand generations":** The Hebrew לְאֶלֶף דּוֹר — literally "to a thousand generation" (singular dor, collective). The LXX renders εἰς χιλίας γενεάς (to thousands of generations, plural). The MT singular is preserved in translation.

**V.20 — tsir'ah (hornets):** Hebrew הַצִּרְעָה — the hornet or wasp. The LXX renders σφηκίαν (wasp-nest). Some interpreters allegorize this as "panic/discouragement" but the literal reading of a stinging insect is preserved.

**V.25 — to'evah:** Hebrew תּוֹעֵבָה — "abomination," a term of strongest ritual disgust. The LXX renders βδέλυγμα (detestable thing). Rendered literally throughout.

**V.26 — double cherem:** The verse employs the verb חָרַם twice in the Hiphil (תַּחֲרִימֶנּוּ — "you shall utterly devote it to destruction"). The object itself becomes cherem by contact. This infectious quality of the ban is preserved in the translation structure. LXX Vat. p.149 confirms the double formulation.

— EMET
